subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multi-digit numerals up to 1,000, 000,000 or 1 billion
indicator_description: Model number quantities up to 1,000,000 using graph sheets and multi-base block
examplars:
1. Ask pupils to model number quantities up to 1,000,000 using graph sheets or multi-base materials (e.g. cube = 1000 unit; rod = 10,000; flat = 100,000 and a block = 1,000,000).
2. Ask pupils to model the number 137,000 shading graph sheet square.
3. Give learners teacher-make token currency notes [¢10, ¢100 & ¢500 notes] on different coloured-paper and ask them work out how many will be required to model given amounts up to ¢1,000,000.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multi-digit numerals up to 1,000, 000,000 or 1 billion
indicator_description: Read and write numbers in figures and in words up to 1,000,000,000
examplars:
1. Play the place value number wheel game: Use the million number wheel to generate 7-digit numbers and represent the number generated on a place value frame.
2. Ask pupils to write given numbers on the expanded form of the number.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multi-digit numerals up to 1,000, 000,000 or 1 billion
indicator_description: Identify numbers in different positions around a given number in a number chart
examplars: 1. Display a number chart in multiples of 1,500 between 10,000 and 50,000 and lead learners identify numbers in different positions around a given number.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multi-digit numerals up to 1,000, 000,000 or 1 billion
indicator_description: Compare and order whole numbers up to 100,000 and represent the comparison using '>, <, or ='
examplars:
1. Learners identify numbers which are 10,000 more or 10,000 less than a given six-digit number.
2. Learners work together in their groups to order a given set of numbers in ascending or descending order verbally and in writing.
3. Give two numbers between 10,000 and 100,000 to each group and encourage learners to say as many things as possible about the two numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multi-digit numerals up to 1,000, 000,000 or 1 billion
indicator_description: Round (off, up, down) whole numbers up to 100,000 to the nearest ten thousands, thousands, hundreds and tens
examplars:
1. Learners round off numbers to the nearest 100,000, 10,000 and 1000.
2. Learners round up and round down to estimate sums and differences.
3. Give two numbers between 10,000 and 1000,000 to each group and encourage learners to compare and say as many things as possible about the two numbers.
4. Ask learners to work together to round up and round down to estimate sums and differences.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multi-digit numerals up to 1,000, 000,000 or 1 billion
indicator_description: Skip count forwards and backwards in 5000s, 10,000s etc. up to and from 1,000,000.
examplars:
1. Put learners into convenient groups. A learner mentions a number and another makes skip count in 5000s or 10000s to include the fifth count.
2. Skip count forwards and backwards by 5000s and between 10000 and 100000, but make an error or leave out a number.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.1.2 Demonstrate understanding of Roman Numerals up to C (i.e. 100)
indicator_description: Recognise Roman Numerals system up to C (i.e. 100)
examplars:
1. Display roman numeral charts (1-100) arranged in sequential order and lead learners to identify the numerals.
2. Have learners match the Roman numerals to the Hindu-Arabic numerals.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.1.2 Demonstrate understanding of Roman Numerals up to C (i.e. 100)
indicator_description: Count and convert Hindu Arabic numbers to Roman numerals up to 100 (C) and vice versa
examplars:
1. Learners read the chart sequentially forwards and backwards, vertically, zig-zag, diagonally and randomly.
2. Give learners a numeral in the Hindu Arabic system and have learners convert to roman numeration and vice versa.
3. Challenge learner to find missing numbers in addition and subtraction sentences involving roman numerals.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.1.3 Demonstrate understanding of factors, multiples and prime numbers from 1 to 100
indicator_description: Determine the HCF and the LCM of two or three numbers using prime factors
examplars:
1. Have learners revise the use of the factor tree method to determine prime factors of any given whole number.
2. Learners use the prime factorization by inspection to determine the HCF by underlining the common factors in each product.
3. Learners use the prime factorization by inspection to determine the LCM and underlining the largest number of factors in each product.
4. Learners place factors in a Venn diagrams to find the HCF and LCM.
5. Learners use the prime factorisation to determine the LCM and HCF of three numbers using table and dividing through by prime factors.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.2.1 Describe and apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties to determine answers for basic multiplication facts to 144 and related division facts
indicator_description: Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties, such as skip counting from a known fact, using doubling or halving, using patterns in the 9s and 11s facts, using repeated doubling or halving, to determine answers for basic multiplication facts to 81 and related division facts.
examplars:
1. Describe the mental Mathematics strategies used to determine a given basic fact.
2. Recall multiplication facts to 81and related division facts.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.2.1 Describe and apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties to determine answers for basic multiplication facts to 144 and related division facts
indicator_description: Apply mental mathematics strategies for multiplication, such as annexing then adding zero halving and doubling using the distributive property
examplars:
1. Determine the products when one factor is a multiple of 10, 100, or 1000 by annexing zero or adding zeros.
2. Apply halving and doubling when determining a given product.
3. Apply the distributive property to determine a given product involving multiplying factors that can be written in the expanded form.
4. Explain the property for determining the answer when multiplying numbers by one or zero.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.2.2 Demonstrate understanding of multiplication of a 2 or 3-digit number by a 2 or 3-digit number.
indicator_description: Multiply multi digit numbers by 2 or 3-digit numbers efficiently
examplars:
1. Multiplication of whole numbers using the “expand and box” method (partial decomposition method).
2. Multiplication of whole numbers using the Column or vertical method.
3. Multiplication of whole numbers using the Distributive property.
4. Multiplication of whole numbers using the lattice method.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.2.3 Manipulate numbers, using basic division fact up to 144
indicator_description: Determine basic division fact up to 81
examplars:
1. Investigate numbers that are multiples of 6, 8, 9 and 11 to know divisibility.
2. Draw a 6 by 6 multiplication chart and use it as a game board to play the 3-in-a-line game.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.2.4 Demonstrate understanding of division of a 2 or 3-digit number by a 1 or 2-digit number
indicator_description: Divide 3-digit numbers by 1-digit number efficiently
examplars: 1. Division as repeated subtraction (using the long division method).

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.2.5 Translate word problems into mathematical sentences and solve
indicator_description: Solve multi step word problems involving the four basic operations
examplars:
1. Learners model mathematical statements from a given word problem involving addition and multiplication and solve using the strategies learnt.
2. Learners model mathematical statements from a given word problem involving division and subtraction and solve using the strategies learnt.
3. Learners role play a given word problem involving addition and multiplication and solve.
4. Learners role play a given word problem involving subtraction and division and solve.
5. Learners model mathematical statements from a given word problem involving division and multiplication and solve.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.2.6 Demonstrate an understanding of integers
indicator_description: Locate, compare and order sets of integers using the number line and symbols '< or >'
examplars: 1. Use number line to help learners to identify integers as opposites of whole numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.2.6 Demonstrate an understanding of integers
indicator_description: Solve simple addition and subtraction problems involving integers (excluding subtraction of negative numbers)
examplars:
1. Use number line to help learners to do addition types.
2. Learners do subtraction types.
3. Solve word problems involving addition resulting in negative integers and relative position in diving.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.2.6 Demonstrate an understanding of integers
indicator_description: Perform simple multiplication with integers
examplars: 1. Use number line to help learners to multiply integers starting from zero and skip/hop to the left or right.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of strategies for comparing, adding, subtracting, multiplying and dividing common, decimal and percent fractions
indicator_description: Compare and order a mixture of fractions: common, percent and decimal fractions (up to thousandths)
examplars:
1. To compare and order a mixture of common, decimal and percent fractions, express them in one form.
2. To compare and order a mixture of common, decimal and percent fractions you can locate the fractions on the number and order them.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of strategies for comparing, adding, subtracting, multiplying and dividing common, decimal and percent fractions.
indicator_description: Add and subtract unlike and mixed fractions
examplars:
1. To add like mixed fractions that are larger than 1, write down the sum of the whole numbers and add the fractions.
2. To subtract like-fractions that are larger than 1, change the mixed fractions into improper fractions and subtract.
3. To add or subtract improper fractions with different denominators, find the Lowest Common Denominator (LCD) and use this to express the equivalent fractions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of strategies for comparing, adding, subtracting, multiplying and dividing common, decimal and percent fractions.
indicator_description: Use models to explain the result of multiplying a fraction by whole number, a whole number by a fraction and a fraction by fraction
examplars:
1. To multiply a whole number by a mixed fraction multiply whole numbers then whole number by fraction or change mixed to improper fraction and multiply.
2. To multiply a fraction by a whole number interpretation as “of”.
3. To multiply a fraction by a whole number first change all into common fractions, then multiply numerators and denominators separately.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of the concept of ratios and its relationship to fractions and to the multiplication and division of whole numbers
indicator_description: Use concrete models and pictorial representations to explain a ratio as a concept that shows the number of times one quantity can be obtained in another and write this symbolically and in its simplest form
examplars:
1. Use concrete objects and/or pictorial representations to explain ratio as a number which tells the number of times a quantity can be obtained in another.
2. Use concrete objects and/or pictorial representations to explain simplest form of a ratio.
3. Solve simple problems that involve ratios and finding total ratios.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of the concept of ratios and its relationship to fractions and to the multiplication and division of whole numbers
indicator_description: Express ratios in equivalent forms, compare and order ratios
examplars:
1. Use the concept of ratio as a fraction to find equivalent ratios that can be easily compared.
2. Solve simple problems that involve simplifying, comparing, finding missing and total ratios.
3. Solve simple problems that involve ratios and finding total ratios.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.4.2 Understand the concept of proportion and its relationship to ratios and rates. Use proportional reasoning and rates to solve real world and mathematical problems.
indicator_description: Use models to explain proportion as a comparison between quantities with equal ratios
examplars:
1. Use mapping diagram to explain the concept of proportion as equal fractions or equivalent ratios.
2. Give learners mappings to identify those that are proportional and those that are not.
3. Work out proportion in given contexts and use them in solving problems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.1.4.2 Understand the concept of proportion and its relationship to ratios and rates. Use proportional reasoning and rates to solve real world and mathematical problems.
indicator_description: Use various strategies to solve proportional reasoning problems involving rates and scales
examplars:
1. Use diagrams to explain the concept of rate a ratio that compares two different quantities measured in different units.
2. Work out rates and use them in solving problems.
3. Use diagrams to explain the concept of scale as a ratio that compares two different sizes – an object and its model.
4. Work out scales and use them in solving problems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Represent a given pattern visually to verify predictions
examplars:
1. Ask learners to examine the pattern made with match sticks.
2. Ask learners to examine the pattern in the table which shows the number of match sticks used in making a pattern of squares.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Write a rule in words and in algebra to represent a given pattern
examplars: 1. Ask learners to state the rules in words and in algebra to represent a given linear patterns.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Describe the relationship in a given table or chart, using a mathematical expression
examplars:
1. Table shows pattern of cost of boxed lunches for students on field trip.
2. Ask learners to write questions based on the pattern for their friends to find answers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.2.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of algebraic expressions
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding of algebraic expressions as mathematical phrases that can contain letters which represent ordinary numbers and operators
examplars:
1. Write algebraic expressions for word problems.
2. Write algebraic expressions for the perimeter of shapes.
3. Write algebraic expressions for the area of shapes.
4. Simplify basic algebraic expressions by grouping like terms.
5. Substitute a value for an unknown into an expression and correctly calculate the answer.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.2.3.1 Solve problems involving single-variable, one-step equations with whole number coefficients.
indicator_description: Identify the unknown in a problem; represent the problem with an equation; and solve the problem concretely, pictorially or symbolically.
examplars: 1. Learners find the value of x in equations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.2.3.1 Solve problems involving single-variable, one-step equations with whole number coefficients.
indicator_description: Create a problem for a given equation.
examplars:
1. Ask students to describe two different stories that the equation 4 − x = 9 could represent.
2. Ask learners to solve puzzle in the figure, by solving the equations in each line.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of prisms
indicator_description: Identify examples of rectangular and triangular prisms in the classroom and the community
examplars:
1. Give learners models of 3-D shapes to examine and describe cross sections.
2. Ask learners to sort 3-D shapes with uniform cross sections.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of prisms.
indicator_description: Construct rectangular and triangular prisms from their nets.
examplars:
1. Give learners rectangular and triangular prisms to open and examine nets.
2. Give learners cardboard to draw net of square prism.
3. Give learners cardboard to draw net of rectangular prism.
4. Give learners cardboard to draw net of triangular prism.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.3.3.5 Describe the position of objects in space using the cardinal points
indicator_description: Tell the position and motion of objects in space using the cardinal points north east, north-west, south-east and south-west
examplars:
1. Pupils describe position and motion of objects using 4 main cardinal points and half-betweens.
2. Pupils describe position of places in a grid from a given location.
3. Ask learners to give directions from Kofi’s house to various locations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.3.3.5 Perform a single transformation (i.e. reflection translation) on a 2D shape
indicator_description: Perform and identify images of a single transformation (i.e. reflection) on 2D shapes in a plane
examplars:
1. Give learners worksheets with incomplete 2D shapes to review drawing images reflected in line outside object.
2. Review properties of images under reflection.
3. Use grid to demonstrate ‘horizontal followed by vertical’ movements called translation.
4. Discuss properties of images under translations.
5. Give learners worksheets to draw images under translation vector given.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.3.3.5 Perform a single transformation (i.e. reflection translation) on a 2D shape.
indicator_description: Identify images of a single transformation (i.e. reflection and translation) of a 2D shapes in a plane
examplars:
1. Ask learners to identify images of reflections of 2D shapes.
2. Ask learners to identify images of translations of given 2D shapes.

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Data Collection, Organization, Presentation, Interpretation and Analysis
content_standard: B6.4.1.1 Create, label, and interpret line graphs to draw conclusions.
indicator_description: Draw a line graph by determining the common attributes (title, axes and intervals) and draw the graph for a given table of values
examplars:
1. Give learners data presented in tables and ask them to determine common attributes and draw line graph.
2. Ask learners to draw line graph for data on water temperature.
3. Ask learners to study line graph and answer questions.

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Data Collection, Organization, Presentation, Interpretation and Analysis
content_standard: B6.4.1.1 Create, label, and interpret line graphs to draw conclusions.
indicator_description: Determine whether a given set of data can be represented by a line graph (continuous data) or a series of points (discrete data) and explain why
examplars:
1. Through discussion guide learners to understand difference between discrete and continuous data.
2. Ask learners to write questions for their friends to read and interpret tables.

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Data Collection, Organization, Presentation, Interpretation and Analysis
content_standard: B6.4.1.1 Create, label, and interpret line graphs to draw conclusions.
indicator_description: Apply understanding of how to create a line graph by using a given table of values (or set of data) to draw a line graph and answer questions based on them to interpret and draw conclusions
examplars:
1. Ask learners to draw more line graphs for given data values.
2. Ask learners to study line graph and answer questions.
3. Ask learners to write questions for their friends to read and interpret line graphs.

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Data Collection, Organization, Presentation, Interpretation and Analysis
content_standard: B6.4.1.2 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data, including questionnaires, interview, observation, experiments, databases, electronic media, etc.
indicator_description: Select a method for collecting data to answer a given question and justify the choice
examplars:
1. Lead learners to discuss and write down how they would make decisions in specific situations.
2. Lead discussion on methods of data collection and ask them to identify method for each situation.

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Data Collection, Organization, Presentation, Interpretation and Analysis
content_standard: B6.4.1.2 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data, including questionnaires, interviews, observations, experiments, databases, electronic media, etc.
indicator_description: Design and administer a questionnaire for collecting data to answer a given question and record the results
examplars:
1. Guide learners to do surveys using Question Form provided.
2. Guide learners to use a table to organize data obtained.

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Data Collection, Organization, Presentation, Interpretation and Analysis
content_standard: B6.4.1.2 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data, including questionnaires, interview, observation, experiments, databases, electronic media, etc.
indicator_description: Design and administer a questionnaire for collecting data to answer given question(s), record the data, analyse and graph the results to solve problems
examplars:
1. Guide learners to use tallies and do a frequency table for each question.
2. Ask learners to study results in frequency tables to draw bar or line graphs and write conclusion.
3. Ask learners to identify questions for survey, do question form, collect data, organize into frequency tables and graph it.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.4.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of probability by identifying all possible outcomes of a probability experiment, determining the theoretical and experimental probability of outcomes in a probability experiment
indicator_description: List the possible outcomes of a probability experiment, such as tossing a coin, rolling a die with a given number of sides, spinning a spinner with a given number of sectors and determine the theoretical probability of an outcome occurring for a given probability experiment
examplars:
1. Through discussion guide learners to understand theoretical vs experimental probability.
2. Guide learners to carry out experiments 100 times using coin and die.
3. Guide learners to use results to work out experimental probability and compare to theoretical.
4. Ask learners to work out experimental probability for specific events.
5. Put results from all groups together and compare.
6. Ask learners observations on whether experimental probability is getting closer to theoretical.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.4.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of probability by identifying all possible outcomes of a probability experiment, determining the theoretical and experimental probability of outcomes in a probability experiment.
indicator_description: Predict the probability of a given outcome occurring for a given probability experiment by using theoretical probability
examplars:
1. Guide learners to carry out experiments spinning 5-sector and 8-sector spinners 100 times.
2. Ask learners to work out experimental probability for specific spinner events.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B6.4.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of probability by identifying all possible outcomes of a probability experiment, determining the theoretical and experimental probability of outcomes in a probability experiment
indicator_description: Explain that the experimental probability approaches the theoretical probability of a particular outcome as the number of trials in an experiment increases
examplars:
1. Put results from all groups together and work out experimental probabilities to compare to theoretical.
2. Ask learners observations as to whether experimental probability is getting closer to theoretical and explain difference.
